title: शनिवार व्रत कथा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनिवार व्रत कथा ॥

ॐ शं शनैश्चराय नमः॥

॥ व्रत विधि ॥
शनिवार का व्रत शनिदेव को समर्पित है। प्रातःकाल स्नान करके काले या नीले वस्त्र धारण करें। शनिदेव या पीपल वृक्ष की पूजा करें। सरसों का तेल, काले उड़द, काले तिल, लोहे का दान करें। एक समय भोजन करें। नमक रहित भोजन विशेष फलदायी है। सायंकाल कथा सुनें।

॥ कथा ॥
एक बार देवताओं की सभा में विवाद हुआ कि नवग्रहों में
सबसे बड़ा कौन है? सभी ग्रहों ने अपनी-अपनी महिमा बताई।
शनिदेव ने कहा — मैं सबसे प्रभावशाली हूँ।
मेरी साढ़ेसाती से कोई नहीं बच सकता।
राजा हो या रंक, सब मेरे प्रभाव में आते हैं॥

यह सुनकर सभी ग्रहों ने कहा — तो सबसे पहले
राजा विक्रमादित्य के पास जाओ और उनकी परीक्षा लो।
राजा विक्रमादित्य न्यायप्रिय और धर्मात्मा राजा थे॥

शनिदेव ने राजा विक्रमादित्य पर अपनी साढ़ेसाती प्रारम्भ की।
पहले राजा का घोड़ा भागा, फिर व्यापारी ने उन्हें चोर समझकर
पकड़वा दिया। राजा के हाथ-पैर काट दिये गये।
एक तेली ने दया करके उन्हें रख लिया।
विक्रमादित्य तेली के यहाँ कोल्हू चलाते रहे॥

एक दिन राजा ने एक राग गाया जो इतना मधुर था
कि नगर की राजकुमारी ने उसे सुना और मोहित हो गई।
राजकुमारी ने विक्रमादित्य से विवाह किया।
विवाह की रात्रि में शनिदेव प्रकट हुए और कहा —
राजन, मैं तुम्हारी धैर्यशीलता से प्रसन्न हूँ।
तुमने कठिन समय में भी धर्म नहीं छोड़ा॥

शनिदेव ने कहा — जो प्राणी शनिवार का व्रत करता है,
काले तिल, सरसों का तेल दान करता है
और मेरी कथा सुनता है, उस पर मेरी कृपा सदा बनी रहती है।
मैं अपने भक्तों को कभी कष्ट नहीं देता॥

विक्रमादित्य के हाथ-पैर पुनः आ गये।
उनका राज्य वापस मिला और वे पुनः सिंहासन पर बैठे।
समस्त प्रजा ने शनिवार का व्रत प्रारम्भ किया
और शनिदेव की कृपा से सुखी रही॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
जो प्राणी श्रद्धापूर्वक शनिवार का व्रत करता है
और इस कथा को सुनता है, उसकी शनि पीड़ा,
साढ़ेसाती और ढैय्या शान्त होती है।
शनिदेव प्रसन्न होकर सुख, समृद्धि और दीर्घायु प्रदान करते हैं।

॥ इति श्री शनिवार व्रत कथा सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shanivar Vrat Katha ||

Om Sham Shanaischaraya Namah.

|| Vrat Vidhi ||
Shanivar Ka Vrat Shanidev Ko Samarpit Hai. Pratahkal Snan Karke Kale Ya Nile Vastra Dharan Karen. Shanidev Ya Peepal Vriksha Ki Puja Karen. Sarson Ka Tel, Kale Urad, Kale Til, Lohe Ka Dan Karen. Ek Samay Bhojan Karen. Namak Rahit Bhojan Vishesh Phaldayi Hai. Sayankal Katha Sunen.

|| Katha ||
Ek Baar Devtaon Ki Sabha Mein Vivad Hua Ki Navagrahon Mein
Sabse Bada Kaun Hai? Sabhi Grahon Ne Apni-apni Mahima Batayi.
Shanidev Ne Kaha — Main Sabse Prabhavshali Hun.
Meri Saadhesati Se Koi Nahin Bach Sakta.
Raja Ho Ya Rank, Sab Mere Prabhav Mein Aate Hain.

Yah Sunkar Sabhi Grahon Ne Kaha — To Sabse Pahle
Raja Vikramaditya Ke Pas Jao Aur Unki Pariksha Lo.
Raja Vikramaditya Nyayapriy Aur Dharmatma Raja The.

Shanidev Ne Raja Vikramaditya Par Apni Saadhesati Prarambh Ki.
Pahle Raja Ka Ghoda Bhaga, Phir Vyapari Ne Unhen Chor Samajhkar
Pakadwa Diya. Raja Ke Hath-pair Kaat Diye Gaye.
Ek Teli Ne Daya Karke Unhen Rakh Liya.
Vikramaditya Teli Ke Yahan Kolhu Chalate Rahe.

Ek Din Raja Ne Ek Rag Gaya Jo Itna Madhur Tha
Ki Nagar Ki Rajkumari Ne Use Suna Aur Mohit Ho Gayi.
Rajkumari Ne Vikramaditya Se Vivah Kiya.
Vivah Ki Ratri Mein Shanidev Prakat Hue Aur Kaha —
Rajan, Main Tumhari Dhairyashilta Se Prasanna Hun.
Tumne Kathin Samay Mein Bhi Dharma Nahin Chhoda.

Shanidev Ne Kaha — Jo Prani Shanivar Ka Vrat Karta Hai,
Kale Til, Sarson Ka Tel Dan Karta Hai
Aur Meri Katha Sunta Hai, Us Par Meri Kripa Sada Bani Rahti Hai.
Main Apne Bhakton Ko Kabhi Kasht Nahin Deta.

Vikramaditya Ke Hath-pair Punah Aa Gaye.
Unka Rajya Wapas Mila Aur Ve Punah Simhasan Par Baithe.
Samast Praja Ne Shanivar Ka Vrat Prarambh Kiya
Aur Shanidev Ki Kripa Se Sukhi Rahi.

|| Phalashruti ||
Jo Prani Shraddhapurvak Shanivar Ka Vrat Karta Hai
Aur Is Katha Ko Sunta Hai, Uski Shani Pida,
Saadhesati Aur Dhaiyya Shant Hoti Hai.
Shanidev Prasanna Hokar Sukh, Samriddhi Aur Dirghayu Pradan Karte Hain.

|| Iti Shri Shanivar Vrat Katha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
शनिवार व्रत कथा में राजा विक्रमादित्य पर शनि की साढ़ेसाती और उनके धैर्य का वर्णन है।

nवग्रहों में श्रेष्ठता के विवाद में शनिदेव ने राजा विक्रमादित्य की परीक्षा ली। साढ़ेसाती में राजा ने अत्यन्त कष्ट सहे — चोर समझे गये, हाथ-पैर कटे, तेली के यहाँ कोल्हू चलाया। किन्तु उन्होंने धर्म नहीं छोड़ा।

शनिदेव उनके धैर्य से प्रसन्न हुए और कहा कि जो शनिवार व्रत करता है, काले तिल-तेल दान करता है और कथा सुनता है, उस पर सदा कृपा रहती है।

विक्रमादित्य का राज्य पुनः प्राप्त हुआ। शनिवार व्रत से साढ़ेसाती, ढैय्या और शनि पीड़ा शान्त होती है।
benefits: Traditional fasting story for Saturday featuring King Vikramaditya
Most powerful remedy for Shani Sade Sati and Dhaiya afflictions
Donating mustard oil, black sesame, and iron pleases Shani Dev
Teaches patience and dharma during difficult times
Shani Dev rewards devotees who maintain faith through hardships
Salt-free food on Saturday enhances the vrat's effectiveness
description: Dedicated to Lord Shani Dev, the Shanivar Vrat Katha (Saturday Fasting Story) (शनिवार व्रत कथा) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Shani Mahatmya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to salt-free food on Saturday enhances the vrat's effectiveness and shani Dev rewards devotees who maintain faith through hardships. It is also said to teach patience and dharma during difficult times. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti. With 14 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shanivar Vrat Katha (Saturday Fasting Story) in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shanivar Vrat Katha and which legend does it tell?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shanivar Vrat Katha (शनिवार व्रत कथा) is the traditional Saturday fasting story dedicated to Lord Shanidev, recited as part of the Shanivar Vrat puja vidhi every Saturday and especially during Sade Sati and Shani-affliction periods. The most beloved katha — preserved in the Shani Mahatmya and the Vikramaditya legend cycle — narrates how Lord Shani once visited the court of the great king Vikramaditya of Ujjain to settle a dispute among the Navagrahas about which planet was supreme. To prove his power, Shani placed Vikramaditya under his Sade Sati, leading the king through years of dramatic suffering — false accusations, the loss of his hands and feet, employment at a tilseller's oil mill — until his unwavering dharma earned Shani's grace and full restoration of his kingdom. (Source: Shani Mahatmya; Vikramaditya legends.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Shani test even great kings like Vikramaditya?
seoFaqs.answer: The deep teaching of the Vikramaditya katha is that Shanidev's Sade Sati spares no one — raja ho ya rank, sab mere prabhav mein aate hain (whether king or pauper, all come under my influence) — because Shani is not a malefic enemy but the supreme cosmic karma-phala-data, the strict but just teacher who delivers to every being precisely the karmic fruits they have earned. Vikramaditya's dharmic patience throughout extreme hardship — never abandoning truth, never blaming Shani, never compromising character — is precisely what earned him Shani's pleased darshan and the blessing: I never harm those who honour my vrat, donate sesame and mustard oil, and listen to my katha. The story teaches the supreme value of dharma-nishtha (steadfastness in righteousness) during hardship. (Source: Shanivar Vrat Katha; Shani Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the proper Shanivar Vrat vidhi (Saturday fasting procedure)?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional Shanivar Vrat vidhi: (1) bathe early in the morning (preferably before sunrise) and wear black or dark blue clothes (Shani's colours), (2) face west or south while doing puja, (3) worship Shanidev's image or murti, or alternatively perform parikrama and abhishekam at a peepal tree (sacred to Shani) with sesame oil, (4) offer black sesame seeds, mustard oil, black urad dal, and an iron object (loha) — donating these to needy persons or to the temple is especially meritorious, (5) eat only one meal in the day, ideally salt-free (namak rahit), (6) listen to or recite the Shanivar Vrat Katha in the evening before breaking the fast, (7) chant the Shani mantra Om Sham Shanaishcharaya Namah 108 times and offer arati. (Source: Shani Mahatmya; vrat-katha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who should observe the Shanivar Vrat and for how long?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shanivar Vrat is especially recommended for: (1) anyone undergoing Sade Sati (the seven-and-a-half year Shani transit over the natal Moon), (2) those in Dhaiya (the smaller two-and-a-half year Ardhashtama or Kantaka Shani transits), (3) those in Shani Mahadasha or Shani Antardasha, (4) those facing chronic illness, debts, court cases or career obstacles (all governed by Shani), (5) those born on Saturday or with Shani as ascendant lord. Traditionally observed for seven, eleven, or twenty-one consecutive Saturdays, or as a lifelong nityapaath. The udyapan (concluding ceremony) is performed after the chosen number of Saturdays with a final formal puja and feeding of seven Brahmins or seven needy persons. (Sources: Shani Mahatmya; classical vrat tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What donations (daan) please Shanidev most on Saturday?
seoFaqs.answer: Shanidev is supremely pleased by donations of objects associated with him: (1) kala til (black sesame seeds) — Shani's sacred grain, most powerful donation; (2) sarson ka tel (mustard oil) — for offering to peepal tree, lighting lamps, or donating; (3) kale urad (black urad dal) — Shani's grain; (4) loha (iron objects) — Shani's metal; (5) kale vastra (black or dark blue cloth) — Shani's garment; (6) kale joote / chappal (black shoes/sandals) — especially for the poor and labourers; (7) chhata (umbrella) — to protect the needy from sun and rain, very pleasing to Shani; (8) feeding of crows (Shani's vahana). Donating these to genuinely needy persons (especially elderly, labourers, scavengers) on Saturday is the most prescribed Shani-shanti remedy. (Source: Shani Mahatmya; classical jyotisha remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Shanivar Vrat Katha effective for relief from Sade Sati?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — the regular weekly observance of Shanivar Vrat (with the proper vidhi: salt-free single meal, black-clothes puja, peepal-tree mustard-oil offering, daan of sesame and oil, listening to the Shanivar Vrat Katha, and chanting Om Sham Shanaishcharaya Namah 108 times) is among the most prescribed remedies in the classical jyotisha tradition for relief from Sade Sati, Dhaiya and Shani-mahadasha afflictions. The katha itself ends with the explicit phalashruti: jo prani shraddhapurvak Shanivar ka vrat karta hai aur is katha ko sunta hai, uski Shani pida, Sade Sati aur Dhaiya shanta hoti hai (whoever observes Saturday fast with faith and listens to this katha — his Shani affliction, Sade Sati and Dhaiya are pacified). It is most effective when combined with Hanuman Chalisa recitation. (Sources: Shanivar Vrat Katha phalashruti; classical Shani-shanti tradition.)
